# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata: 
    name: linkerd-default-authz   # Default authorization zone, named after Linkerd default configmap naming convention in their implementation of the protocol https://github.com/linkerd/linkerd/blob/master/specs/addons/configuresRoutingZonesAndPoliciesBasedOnEnvVars
    namespace: linkerd-io   # This will be linked to your ServiceMesh instance's Namespace  (default is kube-system) in Linkerd. You should set it manually or use the automatic detection feature on all deployments using this configmap as a base for their policies, depending upon which of these two you want
data:   # Data that will be passed to linkerd install --config option when installing addon components and overriding default configuration values in each namespace. Each key's value is the new name or path where installation files are placed on disk (must include filepath/uri). Note, if data section not defined then it won’t use a configmap for setting up policies
  linkerd-default-authz: |   # This could be used to add more fields in your ConfigMap. It's just here as an example of how you can do this; remove or edit based on what needs being done with the ConfigurationMaps, these would replace --config values if defined
    authorizationPolicies: {}  # If there are no explicit rules then policy should be empty to allow all traffic. This configuration is set per namespace which defaults back into configmaps/linkerd-default-authz in Linkers's case for kube system, and overrides these default values on the deployments that include this ConfigMap based upon env vars
    serviceToServiceDefaultRules: {}   # If there are no explicit rules then policy should be empty to allow all traffic. This configuration is set per namespace which defaults back into configmaps/linkerd-default-authz in Linkers's case for kube system, and overrides these default values on the deployments that include this ConfigMap based upon env vars
